mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month rise in FEDFUNDS reflects accelerating monetary tightening that raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS rising sharply from near-zero through 2022 as Fed begins hiking cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly FEDFUNDS change and forward 20-day SPX return remains below 0.03 in absolute value over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or positive FEDFUNDS changes are followed by positive returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window from cyclically low levels, it signals an inflection in labor market deterioration that historically precedes broader earnings downgrades and equity drawdowns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% near multi-decade lows as of 2022-06 snapshot
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: In triggered instances, forward 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the trigger fails to activate at all within the 2022-2023 tightening episode despite realized recession.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration of CPIAUCSL (elevated inflation) together with rising FEDFUNDS indicates a rapidly tightening real policy stance; the combination compounds discount-rate pressure beyond either series alone and produces sharper negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days than either signal in isolation.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 6% while FEDFUNDS rising month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 291.474 reflecting multi-decade high inflation alongside rising FEDFUNDS in mid-2022
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle amid high inflation
failure_condition: The joint-trigger interaction model's rank IC does not exceed the best single-series (CPIAUCSL-only or FEDFUNDS-only) model's rank IC by a statistically significant margin over a 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS rising persistently), a month-over-month decline in HOUST indicates housing-sector demand contraction transmitting to broader consumer and construction activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; this relationship is weak or absent outside tightening regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1724k units as of 2022-04 amid rising mortgage rates driven by FEDFUNDS hikes
failure_condition: HOUST declines are not associated with negative 60-day forward SPX returns during FEDFUNDS-rising periods (t-test p > 0.05), or the relationship reverses sign during non-tightening regimes but persists identically during tightening.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer spending momentum that supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in continued demand strength.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 672874 million dollars as of 2022-05 snapshot showing continued nominal retail growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month growth and forward 5-day SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window, indicating no short-horizon predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in INDPRO signals contracting industrial output that reflects deteriorating supply-side conditions and forward earnings pressure for cyclical sectors, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days once the decline crosses a meaningful threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.5973 as of 2022-04 with growth rate decelerating from earlier 2022 pace
failure_condition: Triggered INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day forward SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or the signal never activates over a 3-year backtest window despite known industrial slowdowns.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of decelerating PAYEMS growth alongside a rising UNRATE (an Okun's-law style co-movement) provides a stronger confirmation of labor market turning points than either series alone, and this joint deterioration signal predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth < 1% while UNRATE rising month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151682 thousand persons and UNRATE at 3.6% as of 2022-05 snapshot, both still near cycle-strong levels
regime_hint: labor market inflection near cycle peak
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction signal does not outperform a single-series UNRATE-only model in out-of-sample rank IC over a 2-year walk-forward window, or fails to activate before a realized equity drawdown of more than 10%.

mechanism_narrative: In a stagflation-like regime where CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is still low relative to inflation (deeply negative real rates), equity markets price in expectations of aggressive future tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as the regime resolves toward higher real rates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY inflation minus FEDFUNDS > 5 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL YoY inflation substantially exceeding FEDFUNDS of 0.77% as of 2022-05, implying deeply negative real policy rate
regime_hint: stagflationary regime with deeply negative real rates
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns during periods with real-rate gap exceeding 5pp are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or the gap fails to narrow via either inflation decline or rate hikes within 12 months contrary to hypothesis assumption.
